summary.goal: Make the app feel as fast as opening a text file in TextEdit — each file opens in a new window at a consistent default size with sub-200ms launch-to-readable-content
summary.description:
The app must feel as fast as opening a text file in TextEdit. Each file opens in a new window at a consistent default size.

## Key Requirements
- macOS native app registered as markdown file handler
- Opens via Finder right-click → Open With
- Each file opens a new window (no window reuse)
- Consistent default window size: ~100-120 character width for optimal monospace readability
- Starts completely fresh each launch — no state persistence (except theme preference)
- Last window close = app quits

## Performance Targets
- Sub-200ms launch-to-readable-content for typical files
- Pre-compiled native binary (Swift) — no runtime startup overhead
- No heavy framework loading on startup
- Small binary size, low memory footprint

## Architecture Notes
- Native Swift binary eliminates Electron/JVM startup penalty
- File passed via macOS Launch Services open event
- No splash screen, no loading indicator — content appears immediately
- Consider pre-warming: keep minimal process footprint for near-instant relaunch

## macOS Integration
- Register as UTI handler for .md, .markdown, .mdown, .mkd files
- Appear in Finder "Open With" menu
- Standard macOS window chrome (traffic lights)
- Code signing and notarization required for distribution

acceptance_criteria.description: App registered as handler for .md, .markdown, .mdown, .mkd files
Launch to readable content in under 200ms for typical files
Each file opens in new window, last window close quits app
Binary is code signed and notarized for distribution

observations: Native Swift binary eliminates startup overhead
File passed via macOS Launch Services open event
No splash screen — content appears immediately
Each file = new window, no window reuse
No state persistence except theme preference
Small binary size, low memory footprint
No heavy framework loading on startup

architect.window_behavior: New window per file, no reuse, no position memory

steps.title: Implement WindowManager for multi-window lifecycle
steps.description: Create a WindowManager that tracks all open document windows. When a file is opened, WindowManager creates a new NSWindow with its associated NSTextView and rendering pipeline. WindowManager maintains a Set of active windows. When a window closes (via NSWindowDelegate.windowWillClose), remove it from the set. When the set becomes empty, call NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil) to quit the app. This implements the 'last window close = quit' behavior.

acceptance_criteria.description: WindowManager tracks all open document windows
Each file open creates a new, independent window
Closing last window triggers app termination
Multiple windows can coexist independently
WindowManager is the NSWindowDelegate for all document windows

architecture.tracking: Set<NSWindow> with weak references or window identifiers

steps.title: Implement default window sizing calculation
steps.description: Create a WindowSizer utility that calculates the default window size based on the active theme's monospace font metrics. Use NSFont.advancementForGlyph() to get exact character width for the theme's font at default size. Target 110 characters visible width (middle of 100-120 range). Width = (char_width * 110) + content_padding + window_chrome. Height = width * 0.75 (4:3) capped at 85% of screen height. Minimum: 60 chars wide, 400pt tall. Maximum: 90% of screen width. Window is centered on the display where the user's cursor is currently located.

acceptance_criteria.description: Window width calculated from font metrics to fit 100-120 characters
Height proportional to width (4:3 ratio), capped at 85% screen height
Window centers on the display containing the mouse cursor
Respects minimum (60 chars, 400pt) and maximum (90% screen) constraints
Recalculates when theme changes (different fonts have different widths)

steps.title: Implement window title with filename and directory path
steps.description: Configure NSWindow title bar to show the filename (e.g., 'README.md') as the main title and the parent directory path as a subtitle. Set the representedURL on the window so macOS shows the document proxy icon (draggable file icon in title bar). The proxy icon allows users to drag the file to other apps or Finder locations — standard macOS document behavior.

acceptance_criteria.description: Window title shows filename (e.g., 'README.md')
Subtitle shows parent directory path
Document proxy icon visible and draggable in title bar
representedURL set correctly on NSWindow

steps.title: Implement Cmd+O Open File dialog
steps.description: When the app is running and user presses Cmd+O, present a standard NSOpenPanel filtered to markdown file extensions (.md, .markdown, .mdown, .mkd). On file selection, open the file in a new window via WindowManager. The open panel should default to the directory of the frontmost window's file (if any), otherwise the user's home directory.

acceptance_criteria.description: Cmd+O opens NSOpenPanel filtered to markdown extensions
Selecting a file opens it in a new window
Panel defaults to frontmost window's file directory
Cancel dismisses panel without side effects

architecture.panel: NSOpenPanel with allowedContentTypes for markdown UTI
architecture.default_directory: Frontmost window's file directory, else home
architecture.multiple_selection: Allowed — each file opens in separate window

steps.title: Implement drag-and-drop file opening on Dock icon
steps.description: Register the application to accept file drops on its Dock icon. This is handled automatically by macOS when the app's Info.plist declares document types (from UTI registration) and the AppDelegate implements application(_:open:). Verify that dragging one or more .md files onto the Dock icon opens each in a separate window. Also support drag-and-drop onto the app icon in Finder and Applications folder.

acceptance_criteria.description: Dragging .md files onto Dock icon opens each in a new window
Multiple files dragged simultaneously open as multiple windows
Drag onto app icon in Finder/Applications also works

architecture.note: If UTI registration and file-open handler are correct, Dock drag-and-drop works automatically
